summary: Iteration_9 verifies the deployment-readiness performance refactor in /app/backend/server.py (bulk_write/insert_many for process_mining_earnings + check_expired_miners, batch user_map for /api/activity/recent). BACKEND ONLY testing per request. 11/11 pytest passed in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_9_perf_refactor.py. Smoke: /api/auth/login, /api/wallet/balance, /api/miners/list, /api/store/miners all 200 for koalatest. /api/activity/recent: returns 200 with well-formed {activities:[], count:int}; items contain type/user_name/timestamp (+miner_name+hash_rate for purchase or amount for cashout); user_name is obfuscated (e.g. 'J*** D**'); empty-list path returns count=0 with no server error. process_mining_earnings: with one active 5000 GH/s miner, the user's bitcoin_balance AND total_earnings both increased after a 16s wait (Δ=9.44e-12 BTC over ~3 ticks, matching the 5435e-15/5s per-GH rate), and >=2 fresh 'earning' transactions with correct shape (user_id, transaction_type='earning', amount>0, description, miner_id, created_at) were inserted via insert_many. check_expired_miners: an active miner's time_remaining decreased from 23.993 -> 23.990 hours over 11s (bulk_write path) AND a synthetic miner with past expires_at was correctly flipped status='active' -> 'expired' with time_remaining=0 within one 5s tick. APScheduler logs show both jobs 'executed successfully' on every 5s tick with no errors throughout the run. No functional regressions detected.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py check_expired_miners (line 321-374): correctly uses field projection {_id,user_id,miner_type,name} + .limit(1000) on expired-find, and projection {_id,expires_at} + .limit(1000) + UpdateOne bulk_write(ordered=False) for the active time_remaining update. LGTM. Minor note: the deactivation loop is still a per-row miners_collection.update_one inside the for-loop on lines 332-335; this was NOT batched into the bulk_write. Acceptable because expired count is typically small (capped at 1000) and each row also triggers a send_push_notification, but could be optimized further in a future pass.

server.py process_mining_earnings (line 376-422): correctly projects {_id,user_id,hash_rate,name}, builds miner_ops/user_earnings/transactions in-memory, then issues exactly THREE writes: bulk_write(miner_ops), bulk_write(users_collection $inc per user), insert_many(transactions, ordered=False). Per-user aggregation via user_earnings dict correctly collapses multi-miner users into one $inc. ordered=False is safe here (independent writes). LGTM.

server.py /api/activity/recent (line 4540-4615): correctly does single users_collection.find({_id:{$in:user_ids}}, {_id:1,name:1}) into user_map dict, then dict-lookup inside the purchase + withdrawal loops -- N+1 eliminated. Field projections on both purchases and withdrawals find(). Wrapped in try/except returning {activities:[], count:0} on failure. LGTM.
Carryover: premium-mining-app.tsx still 5400+ lines and missing testID coverage; not in scope of this iteration.

success_rate.frontend: N/A - frontend testing not in scope this iteration

seed_data_creation: Inside the test session: ensure_active_miner fixture confirmed koalatest had a pre-existing active miner (Daily Free Miner ~24h) so no synthetic insert was needed. The test_expired_miner_gets_deactivated test transiently inserts a synthetic miner '_id=TEST_expire_*' with past expires_at, waits one scheduler tick to confirm status flips to 'expired', then deletes it in a finally block. After the run the DB is in the same state as before (test user keeps its accrued earnings since cleanup of natural mining accrual is intentional). No production data mutated beyond the natural bitcoin_balance/total_earnings/total_earned increments that would have happened anyway from the always-on scheduler.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_9 perf refactor is fully verified: bulk_write/insert_many in process_mining_earnings and check_expired_miners both produce correct user-balance and per-row state mutations; batch user_map in /api/activity/recent serves correctly. APScheduler jobs healthy throughout (5s cadence, executed successfully every tick). Note for tooling: there is NO general /api/transactions endpoint exposed -- 'earning' transactions can only be observed via direct MongoDB query against db.transactions, or indirectly via /api/wallet/balance.today_earnings. Use MongoDB direct access (MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB=bitcoin_mining_db) for any future earnings validation. Earnings rate is tiny (5000 GH/s -> ~1.57e-12 BTC per 5s) so always assert with full float precision (8+ decimals) or wait >=15s for a visible delta.
rca_of_issue: No bug found. Refactor is correct end-to-end.
